subject: Re: MCore crashing at --iter 0
body:
Thanks Alister for the quick response. I went to 1.5binning and smaller box 
size and it is working now. 

On Tuesday, June 3, 2025 at 7:45:02 PM UTC-7 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Hamid,
>
> cuFFT internal error is likely out of memory…
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>
> On Jun 3, 2025, at 19:26, Hamidreza Rahmani <hami...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
> ﻿
>
> 1d 
> <https://forum.image.sc/t/mcore-crashes-at-iter-0/113122?u=hamidreza_rahmani>
>
> Hi all,
>
> I am trying Warp2.0 and following the instructions from Warp
>
>
> Quick Start: Tilt Series - Warp, M and friends 
> <https://warpem.github.io/warp/user_guide/warptools/quick_start_warptools_tilt_series/#setup-in-mtools>
>
> Everything is working great and I can extract particles either from Warp 
> or pyTOM template-matching and reaching 8A using relion 5.
>
> I have no problem running Mtools create_population, MTools create_source, 
> and MTools create_species.
>
> But when I run
> MCore --population m_refinement/pytom_clean_cls2.population --iter 0
> I get this error:
> Creating directories... Done Spawning workers... Done Preparing for 
> refinement – this will take a few minutes per species Preparing refinement 
> requisites... 1/1 Performing refinement bash: line 1: 793093 Killed 
> /home/janlevinson/micromamba/envs/warp/bin/WarpWorker -d 0 -p 0 --pipe 
> /tmp/WarpWorkerPipe_1_793075_548019137 -s > worker0.out 2> worker0.err 
> Preparing population for data source HRR036...Done Loading gain reference 
> for HRR036... Done Refining all series in data source... 0/9Unhandled 
> exception. System.NotImplementedException: The method or operation is not 
> implemented. at MCore.MCore.WorkerDied(Object sender, EventArgs e) in 
> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1723683627331/work/MCore/MCore.cs:line 
> 597 at Warp.WorkerWrapper.ReportDeath() in 
> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1723683627331/work/WarpLib/WorkerWrapper.cs:line 
> 255 at Warp.WorkerWrapper.<StartHeartbeat>b__14_0() in 
> /usr/share/miniconda/envs/package-build/conda-bld/warp_1723683627331/work/WarpLib/WorkerWrapper.cs:line 
> 189 bash: line 1: 793187 Aborted (core dumped) 
> /home/janlevinson/micromamba/envs/warp/bin/WarpWorker -d 0 -p 0 --pipe 
> /tmp/WarpWorkerPipe_2_793075_287069214 -s > worker0.out 2> worker0.err 
>
> I trying the debug mode and --perdevice_preprocess 1 --perdevice_refine 1 
> --perdevice_postprocess 1 just in case it is a GPU problem I get a 
> worker0.out that is empty and a worker0.err that has
> terminate called after throwing an instance of 'std::runtime_error' 
> what(): cuFFT error: CUFFT_INTERNAL_ERROR at /usr/share/miniconda/envs
> /package-build/conda-bld/warp_1723683627331/work/NativeAcceleration/gtom
> /src/FFT/FFT.cu:23 
>
> Any ideas what is happening? I didn’t face this problem when I was testing 
> 1 tomogram but I am not sure if that is relevant.
>
> Best,
> Hamid
>
> -- 
> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google Groups 
> "Warp" group.
> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send an 
> email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.
> To view this discussion visit 
> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/0ae06d4f-936e-4986-a096-44a35b5baeb3n%40googlegroups.com 
> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/0ae06d4f-936e-4986-a096-44a35b5baeb3n%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
> .
>
>
